figures.caption: Fig. 3. Mobile Interface. The custom Phone2Act Android application featuring configurable ROS 2 topics.

Fig. 4. Phone2Act Architecture. The system decouples the smartphone input from the robot hardware via a central, agnostic planner and standardized ROS 2 topics (phone2act/target pose, phone2act/gripper cmd, and phone2act/robot feedback).

Fig. 5. Open-Loop Evaluation of Groot N1.5 trained on Phone2Act data. The predicted actions (orange) closely track the ground truth teleoperation data (blue), indicating successful learning of control signals.

Fig. 6. Real-world execution of the learned policy. Task: ”Pick up the ball and place it in the purple basket.” The Dobot CR5 smoothly executes the sequence: approaching, grasping the target, and placing it into the designated basket.
